channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 103 replies — 412.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 103 • Updated: 2026-06-05 10:21 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62316897
>>62316850
also anon you really should get another source of income, if there are bigger players trying to drag this out, and some of them want to do so for accumulation purposes, situations like yours are exactly what they're doing it for, because they know people will get comfortable thinking "surely it has to happen soon" and start living off their stack, selling directly to their wallets.

Not shilling at all, but just saying, don't let them win if you actually believe in your investment.

62316939
>>62316876
> its trusting that there aren't bigger fish trying to ensure CLL runs out of runway so they can swoop into the wreckage with their own solution
i never thought of this, the factthat CLL relies so muc on their preminted token actually makes them vulnerable, that's a very interesting POV i never thought of, thanks so much man, so the price suppression is to prevent CLL from achieving their goals, ...woahhhh, this opens up a whole new world of thinking in my head ffs this might be why BTC shits the bed every time link tries to moon or gets a news. this is actually crazy.

>the other reason to believe in link is they have their own huge insitutional backers
this! yeah i agree with that especially knowing that sergey was a nobody russian young lad, but he was shaking hands with the biggest, how come schwab back then mentioned smartcontract.com when they were nobodies? also all that intimacy with SWIFT, makes me think that chainlink can't be replaced atleast in the eyes of financial institutions, there is a strong lobby pushing chainlink sergey wasn't picked by chance, there is some shit we don't actually know.

> we are just guppies hoping to ride the right whale
yeah man, i totally feel that 100%

>Its why I never tell anyone to buy link, I know its a massive risk
we are on the same boat, and we think alike,it feels good to know there are still real linkies on biz, i stopped posting for years here because of the jeep spam and the timer they added with email validation and shit, they reduced the timer to 60 seconds so it kinda helps to post again, but i'm really glad to have spoken with you man thanks

>if the price moons the rewards will be small, potentially much smaller than current staking
that's because you are denominating the rewards in LINK, KEK, i just want to live off my staking rewards, so obviously if link pumps the rewards will be much higher in $ and less in LINk yeah i get you lmao

62316982
>>62316897
oh yeah i know, but i have a strategy, basically i hold on the rewards until 2029, i sold enough at literally 30$ (it was my target i set a limit order and it got filled, i didn't know it would be the top of this cycle ) to survive until 2029, i'll let my links staked until 2029 and i'll sell again when we're above 25 in 2029 to ensure i'll live to see 2034, so basically my strategy is to wait for the bull, sell the rewards there, (enough to hold 4 years) and i keep repeating that.

i have enough to hold till 2029 thanks to the build rewards also, i'm safe till 2029, i hope we go beyong 50$ in the next bull, but right now i don't need to worry about crypto at all, i don't give a fuck about the bear

62317017
I figured this out in 2022.
If link really was what it was shilled to be, it wouldnt be trading at $7 and sergey wouldnt be dumping millions at even $5, if sergey really believed in his project and had insider knowledge that it would vastly appreciate, he wouldnt be dumping so much. it goes against human nature.
Dumping to fund development is a lie, what is the $ amount of total dumped tokens at this point? $7 Billion dollars?
It doesnt cost anywhere near that to fund a small software company, and no one ever asks, where did all this money go?
If I had to guess the majority of it was embezzled into offshore bank accounts in typical Russian oligarch fashion. Sergey probably has a vast fortune complete with lambo collections and penthouses in the Maldives.
7 BILLION dollars...
If chainlink has 1000 employees, how much does he have to be paying them per year to burn through that much money....

62317065
>>62317017
i did the math before (chatgpt did with some guidelines)
basically running the company costs between 160 - 290 Million $ / year check pic related for explanation. and if you look at how many tokens they sell on average, it's almost 300M$ which is close to the higher estimate. so yeah they actually burn that money, also chatgpt didnt account for network subsidy and build incubator or smartcon n shit, also, chainlink made multiple aquisitions, and they never revealed how much they paid for those acquisitions, recently they bought town crier, deco and recently rhombus, they prolly paid millions of $ and they will probably make more acquisitions

62317093
>>62316655
>what about the new dead horse we're beating?
just two more years

62317097
>>62316876
>>62316939
thank you both for doing more FUD than us FUDchads ever could lmao

62317143
>>62316982
>>62316939
That's smart (your strategy of keeping afloat I mean), and I agree its sad how botted/jeeted this website has become. Not to mention psychos like adem that have to spam the board 24/7 (I'm shocked he hasn't popped into this thread yet).

Thing is, if they continue to dump at the same rate, its all going to be done by 2029/2030 so you'll know by then. At that point its sink or swim, if chainlink hasn't gotten off the ground with staking 1.0 by then, there's nothing left for them.

I do worry sometimes that other players are manipulating the price to sink this. I've seen people say Sergey's doing it but that makes no logical sense, if he could do that he'd just inflate it and make himself rich. To me there's two reasons for suppression, neutral actors who understand what's being built but that it isn't built yet, and their best long term strategy to accumulate is to keep the price down. And bad actors, who understand link is funding development, and by keeping the price down they can potentially sabotage link on a long enough timeline. The other is bad actors who aren't necessarily trying to replace link, but don't want to see it get retail interest because it fucks with their bottomline, such as CZ and anyone who only views the market as a casino.

62317153
>>62317097
we are linkies, we suffer and we know why we are suffering, the difference between us and any other bagholder is that we know what we are holding and we arn't deluded, we know risks, and the rewards, we've been holding for almost 9 years now, nothing will make us sell, not even reality

62317156
>>62315242
oh cool adem's here. serious question why do you live on this board 24/7? i and many other long time holders only post like once a week, or month even at this point. why do you do it?

62317182
>>62317143
you're very smart, because while i read you i have thoughts that come up, and you answer those thoughts. bene lurking 4chan since 2007 or some shit i've learnt over the years to spot smart / legit people, and you are one, it's rare to meet good people on 4chan back in early days of crypto we used to meet alot of people like you too bad now we only have dumb fucks like these guys
>>62317097
>>62315242
also yeah it's kinda cringe that they keep fudding for absolutely no reason for all these years i don't even see what their goal is, my goal is to make the cll team stress on twatters so they don't feel like we are happy with them, so they stfu and focus on working.
what's these guys goals? probablmy grifters from xrp or some other shitcoin

62317281
>>62313738
You pic is such a retard faggot argument. So what if you are right that they are suppressing chainlink? You’re still poor as fuck lmao.

FYI Xlm had no problem pumping on the exact same news chainlink had. Your coin is a piece of shit

62317289
>>62316251
So why are you buying chainlink you fucking retard lmao

62317437
Ada baggies in absolute shambles. Linkies celebrating are the great new news. Love to see it.

62317656
>>62316776
>>62316850

It has nothing to do with trusting Sergey per se. It’s the fact that this entire behemoth that is Link has been unfolding for years. You autists found all the breadcrumbs, retards like me trusted you and bought in, and it just takes time for the entire system to adjust. So whatever. I’ve never been worried once about anything.

And society sucks now anyway. And it sucks because the people suck. If things didn’t suck… and life was still good like in 2010 or whatever, and we got rich…it wouldn’t have been a remarkable achievement because things were going fine and there were a lot of rich people and people doing relatively well. But since Covid… seeing how all the trash behaved and how worthless most people are…. And also seeing now that these very people are losing their jobs and shit and the stock market and everything else is poised to collapse…. To bring in this new system that Link facilitates. And we get to be on top while the trash get fucked. It’ll be a lot more meaningful cuz we will be able to dab.

62317686
>>62317143
>if he could do that he'd just inflate it and make himself rich

He’s already rich you dumbfuck. The carrot on the stick for him isn’t going from 500x’ing his net worth. It’s the stature he gains from it. And everyone knowing that he’s the Steve Jobs of how the new financial system works. Who knows tho. He’s so autistic he probably doesn’t even care about that.

62318008
>>62316655
>98% of Bitcoin mining rewards come from Satoshi's uncirculating supply.
>The absolute vast majority of cryptos have a similar "incentivisation" scheme.
>You are clueles a clueless ape.

Yes, every chain has a minting/burning protocol. The BTC network works by miners racing for calculate a hash that meets a specific criteria and the first to find a valid hash for a block is rewarded with X bitcoin. The protocol participants agree on the reward amount and consequently the inflation rate. More importantly, this is THE ONLY WAY to obtain new BTC; The BTC out in market came from miners selling. Nowhere else. More importantly, miners are not going to sell BTC below operating costs else they would not be able to maintain operations. These factors lead the BTC and consequently the BTC price to be COUPLED TO THE USAGE OF THE NETWORK.

Chainlink on the other hand is already premined 1 Billion from the start and allocated 350M for market purchase. Yes, Chainlink allocated 300M/350M for node incentivization but they're at the point where DONs are profitable. When they setup price feeds, users (the ones who requested for the specific feeds) pay in USD. CLL dictates the price of the price feeds, not the nodes in the DONs. Chainlink pays out to the nodes, not the from market but from their team wallet. There's no incentive to purchase Link tokens to access Chainlink services (the really valuable ones). If there's no incentive to buy Link to access services and Payment Abstraction isn't ubiquitous across all of Link's services even in the services where you have to option to pay with a native currency onchain, it won't be automatically converted to Link from the market. This means that usage of the network does not cause buy pressure on the network. With node operators unable to dictate prices, the work they do is paid from company wallets, how is the link token coupled to the market again?

I await your response, Fishy.

62318054
>>62316724
>Yeah, I get it. I know. But my point is that BUILD is also one of those initiatives that effectively dilutes us and further dissociates the token from the network.
And I will tell you again it does not. It is an investment. Not lost sales or even dilution of the token circulation. I am telling you this as someone who is strongly considering entering the program himself. Stop doing this stupid crap. Investors are not using CRE/pricefeeds/CCIP ("but I use transporter-"SHUT UP), retail is never going to use CRE/pricefeeds/CCIP. It is developers that use CRE/pricefeeds/CCIP. Whether through creating bridges like transporter ("er, Chainlink built-" SHUT UP), integrating CCIP into their dapp, or orchestrating a bunch of stuff in the backend. But they are using Chainlink's services with the purpose of serving your needs in whatever problem the protocol aims to solve. So if they're successful and popular, Chainlink's network gets more usage and becomes more valuable. Problem is, developing is not easy. Creating a successful product is not easy. You need to build out and you need to market. BUILD is a delightful program in that Chainlink is will work out some cheaper fees with startups but they put them in (You)r direction in hopes that (You) along with other community members may be interested in becoming customers of those protocols. Once they get the ball rolling that boosts the value of their protocol, their token, and of course the Oracle network value.

continued....

62318066
>>62318054
SCALE you can argue and complain that they're not making chains go to the market to purchase Link to pay for the integration. I'm personally not opposed to the setup now because at the end of the day Chainlink is a business and other chains are businesses so there's going to be some Quid pro quo give and take action to get into each others ecosystem in the first place, scratch my back and I scratch yours. Though at this point Chainlink is in the position to start making late stragglers pay in Link.

The real issue again is the price feeds and valuable services not being directly tied to the link tokens on market via buy pressure. The GMX yearly fee and general institutional Link dealings also fall into this category as it's active Link network usage. This should be solved by the Payment Abstraction system but the PAs isn't automated (for instance, the CCIP payments made by native tokens accumulated is sitting on a contract and hasn't converted to link from the market yet) and isn't tied to all Chainlink network services.

Really you're waiting on Staking 1.0 to come out where nodes will have to be required to hold X amount of tokens to qualify for some job and for the banks to start flying with their blockchain revolution via TradFi finally getting to go to production with their smart contract efficient workflows and the new NFT market, Tokenized real world assets. They're waiting on clarity from the Clarity Act. Until then token not needed, and linkies exist to suffer.

62318124
>>62316876
>That's my biggest fear, that some other entity knows they can't compete on the current playing field, so they just draw out the timeline, via shorting the token and other financial games, to the point CLL can't do it, then in 5 years they've built their own. The reason nobody can compete with chainlink now is network effect, but if you destroy the current landscape and start from zero that doesn't matter.

You don't have to worry about this. If they destroy the current landscape, people will be too demoralized to to learn about this new Chainlink competitor's tech, much less learn to integrate the tech. It took LayerZero getting hacked and people finding out the keys that got hacked were shitcoin farming before people finally did their "extensive security review to determine Chainlink's CCIP was the correct fit for their protocol". No doubt this space has brilliant people, but they're not 5D chess players that master secret boss schemer would need to coordinate around.

62318167
>>62318124
Nah man, there are really very few brilliant people in crypto. I worked in the industry for years, most people are average intellect or lesser than, but what's actually worse is nobody has any real work/business experience. I'm sure that'll change with time since as a result they literally all built failed/failing products, but I got the fuck out because I was so far beyond every person I met re: my work experience and I was learning absolutely nothing except how easy it is to outright scam people.

62318173
>>62318167
I admit, I had to rewrite my post I was about to fling a ton of slurs at the industry. I don't understand how people don't see it's a problem that you can go to a crypto conference, and not spend any crypto at all or interact with the blockchain in a meaningful way that makes you think, "holy cow this is the future". But I did go to a conference where one event was a scam event that stole someone's ETH.

62318186
>>62318173
In other words, I was being extremely nice in saying the industry had brilliant people. There are, just not enough to say the least.

62318188
>>62318173
At one point in my crypto "career" I wound up employed by scammers -- obviously didn't know going into it, but it became clear probably less than a year in at which point I started fast looking for my exit. They had raised 8 figs from VCs, were extracting 7 figs left and right from other crypto projects by promising them things they had no intention of building, and all these other crypto projects literally just gave us money without any contractual obligations. The founders would turn around afterwards and just say "yea, we're not going to build that", and really they never built anything. Whole operation was capital extraction, purely. But I almost couldn't blame them, because I started to realize that was 95%+ of the industry, which is why retards sitting on 9-10 figs happily gave away 7 figs to get a couple of tweets. If they could pass as "legitimate" in the eyes of retail, it drastically increased their chances of dumping enough tokens to pay for those $1-2M and then some. The whole game was either fleecing retail or fleecing the people fleecing retail. Crazy shit.

62318952
>>62318008
>The BTC network works by miners racing for calculate a hash
The Chainlink network works by nodes putting external data onchain.

>The BTC out in market came from miners selling
How is this different from Sergey selling?
Or Sergey distributing to nodes who then sell?

>miners are not going to sell BTC below operating costs
Literally any business will avoid selling at a loss.

These factors are what you claim ties the price of BTC to the usage of the network. You're a seriously stupid person.

62319004
>>62317281
>Your coin is a piece of shit
The market pumped XLM even though DTCC is explicitly pursuing "all" chains, and Stellar is just one of literally hundreds.
The market did not pump Link even though DTCC is explicitly using Chainlink in an exclusive fashion to connected those hundreds of chains.

Sounds like the piece of shit here is "the market", not the coin.

62319293
>>62312614
>>62312653
>>62312685
H1DR4 could be an idea for this task

62319302
>>62319004
LMAOOOO this nigga is either a paid shill or a schizophrenic baggie. Anyways price + chart cuckold

62319405
>>62319302
You're just proving my point: this market is a piece of shit.
It pumps XLM, but not Link.
It pumps Toncoin on a fucking name change, but not Link.
Absolute dogshit like BNB, XRP, Doge, Cardano, ... are still top fixtures.
etc.

There's a very good reason Bitcoin is down 50% while the stock market is at ATH.

62319468
wow, a thread with organic conversation. Based anons. Have a bump from yours truly

item.title: I genuinely cannot comprehend how BTC enthusiasts cant look at that graph and see it happening right before their eyes. — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I genuinely cannot comprehend how BTC enthusiasts cant look at that graph and see it happening right before their eyes.

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-05 10:22 UTC

OP

I genuinely cannot comprehend how BTC enthusiasts cant look at that graph and see it happening right before their eyes. Can you not see that every top is lower and every bottom is lower? You are all doubling down on an asset that already peaked. There is no parabolic gains left for you. BTC is likely to roll over. If you dont want to actually accept this likely reality, at least position down.

Early replies (first 3)

62319022
>>62319015
then I shall die as one of them

62319023
>>62319015
Past performance does not guarantee future results

62319026
40K to 160k is a x3

only retards hold

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62319150
>>62319015
Your chart is wrong. It did not account for USD inflation, which became extreme since 2020.

62319380
>>62319015
>There is no parabolic gains left for you
They're logarithmic. Powers of -2. Next one is ~1.4X, so 175k.

62319469
>>62319380
Correct. Still a good asset to own once it bottoms.

item.title: It's understandable investing in defense. — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
It's understandable investing in defense.

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-05 10:25 UTC

OP

It's understandable investing in defense. But how do you justify the military using pussified weapons (e.g drones)? Where is the honor in killing a man from your basement like a lil sweaty boy playing COD or BF?

Early replies (first 3)

62319393
>>62319376
>bows are for pussies
>guns are for pussies
>armored vehicles are for pussies
>artillery is for pussies
And now
>drones are for pussies

LMAO, mankind never changes. Same shit being regurgitated by low IQ copers for thousands of years now.
OP, rejoice, you were today years old when you found out you are a normie

62319394
This. Murder and rape can be honorable but only when done in self defense.

62319396
>>62319394
First you need to bow before you rape someone, else its barbaric.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62319439
poor israeli soldiers, they did nothing wrong

62319482
>>62319376
>just let ziggers and rageheads fuck you up
it's called "defense" sweaty

item.title: BTC to ZEC poverty rotation — 17 replies — 15.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC to ZEC poverty rotation

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-05 09:20 UTC

OP

>bitcoin bulliever since 2021
>all in at 50k avg
>diamond handed for 5 years
>absolute sickening performance since 10/10 has shaken me to my core
>saylor selling fud
>quantum fud
>all the hot money moved to ai
>missed out on infinity gains holding this warm dogshit
>but zcash has been running hot
>it's saylorless, quantumproof, privacy narrative being pushed by all the vcs and kols, ai agents are gonna want sovereign private cash to buy compute with, bla bla bla
>fit of rage
>say fuck it
>sell my 5 year old btc at 66k for a meager 30% gain
>all in zec at $590
>24 hours later
>infinite money glitch disclosed
>-30% in a day

I am now officially poorer than I was in 2021. I hate crypto. I hate /biz/. I hate saylor. I hate zooko. I hate myself. I fucking hate everyone. Fuck this trash fire of an asset class and I hope everyone involved with creating it gets sent to the eternal virtual torture dungeon when the singularity comes. I will never retire, I will never escape the underclass, and it's all because I was retarded enough to believe in "anonymous digital cash" as an investment thesis.

Early replies (first 3)

62318313
>>62318304
tell me you are larping

62318322
>>62318304
Literally nobody that has spent more than a month on /biz/ would ever touch zcash least of all someone here for 5 years

62318345
>>62318313
I wish I could, man. I really do.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62318352
>>62318304
You deserve this. Your hubris got the best of you. You thought your goy gold was the money ticket, when it was really a new set of shackles. It's over for you. Into the ground you go, the world needs you to shovel coal in the wage pits to fuel the AI datacenters.

62318359
>>62318304
>privacy narrative being pushed by all the vcs and kols
yep better buy so they get rich...zioncash

62318375
>>62318345
i understand your pain . bought the top in 2021. at -0% nominally you still did better tthan 90% in crypto thats the bright side. new opportunities will arise. dont emo-yolo now (stocks i mean...) until a pink wojak holocaust on s&p

62318381
>>62318304
I made $33,000 off $1200 in 2020. Watch it go down to $800 cus fell for the diamond hands maymay.
You’ll be okay friend.

62318391
i sold btc just before the crapto crashout in october now im up so much nothing matters.youll get there. buy or dca vwce or s&p trust me youll beat most poeople & (importantly) yourself

62318450
ah geez dude

62318465
>>62318304
bogged

62318498
>>62318304
>"it’s running hot so I buy it"
>buys the top
>gets rekt
>does it again and again

May I suggest you to go all-in on AI stocks? But only after the SpaceX IPO.

62318503
>>62318498
This, elun mask is flippin pancakes in space!

62318506
it is a temporary blip. it will go up again

62319247
>>62318304
I am 20k usd down from 2021 cuz I got rugged on kda but I think will recover this year. The tip I can give you is never give up and just buy more

62319280
>>62318304
Thanks for playing

62319291
>>62318304
>Zec was always a trojan horse
>institutions came to tame you all, killing memes with shit like pump
>hype is lead by them btw
>want to completely demoralize the old players by baiting them into ruin
you got warned by people like me years ago, I could have told (You) if the faggots here didnt kill this board. you still had the general here saying zec was a trap. you just didnt read.

62319296
H1DR4 could be an idea

item.title: 65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC — 52 replies — 14.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-06-05 06:54 UTC

OP

>disregard dwarves
>disregard doomerfags
>if you bought the rally, hold
>if you didn't, enjoy the discount

Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value. You can buy groceries with BTC, you can't buy it with rock collections or shitcoins. And ever less the fiat bullshit can buy groceries.

>but BTC is dumping to zero--

Zoom out.

Bitcoin is the future of currency and if you haven't realized yet, you are either a fool, a scammer, or a paid shill.

Buy the fucking dip and stop selling the bottom.

‐ Yours truly, the BUYROG, pmetalsbane.

Early replies (first 3)

62309508
>>62309503
proof??

62309515
This thread now is the 64k waiting room

62309535
>>62309508
I don't need proof. I made money for everyone who bought the previous dip, who were considering cashing out and start a rock collection. And this time it isn't going to be any fucking different.

ETF returns are pathetic. PM is not investment. Bonds are tanking.

Buy BTC.

Let's get rich together.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62312604
Rainbow pattern!

62312613
Rainbows all the way!

62312656
>>62312595
Americans just got back from work and started dumping after seeing the mess left by institutional and Euro traders

62313701
>>62309503
>Bitcoin is the future of currency
I do not want to hold currency, I want to hold values that I need to live, and diversified shares in upcoming industries. Currency I hold very briefly, for a few weeks, to buy food and stuff.

62313706
>>62309503
61k btw, -6% below the bottom, within hours, 12% below last cycle top, in the spring of the bear year. I smell saturation.

62313723
kek baggie

keep spamming /biz/ maybe Bitcoin will pump by +1%

62313736
>>62309503
>stateless
lol/lmao
did not read

62313815
BTC is now lower than the 2021 top

62313877
>>62313815
>BTC is now lower than the 2021 top


TOP Signal
>20K will be the bottom this year!!

62313967
>>62312142
Well that room got closed when I got there. Seems you are in charge here. Where do I go now?

62314072
>>62313967
Please take a seat in the 60k waiting room and enjoy a delicious free drink.

62314074
65k is my bottom nice long wick on the monthly.

62314089
>>62309559
Bobo is your friend without him everything would be too expensive.

62314092
>>62311487
Building momentum.

62314094
I feel bad for Bitkiddies. They need to always make up a new narrative like iem weirdo shilling you his product on the street and hope you have a 2 year memory so you don't remember all the all the other ways he tried to shill this product to you.


Meanwhile with Gold, all you need to do is look at the news, read world bank headlines and just the general macros that haven't changed in forever. I don't even feel sad or mad at our Bitkiddies anymore. Just say "I have no money" whole walking into the gold miner store.

62314100
>>62314094
Dude youre married on a porn site go reload that shill else where

62314120
>>62314072
The interesting thing is that it broke 75k so easily end of January, so I sold the bounce. I thought 75 is the new Trump value added bottom. But it just teared through it like nothing, now it is almost at the usual bottom just below the last cycle top, weekly chart mostly maxed out, in the most bullish stock market that ever existed. Like all the institutional ideas are of zero value. It seems people really looked at saturation, or found that there aren't any rich buyer groups left to scam into buying the next top.

62314163
>>62314100
Isn't it weird how your "money" is going to be hacked by quantum computers in under 3 years?

62314177
um

62315194
IT IS ALREADY BACK TO 64K AND NORMALIZE ABOVE THE BOTTOM.

THR BUYROG REPEATS ITSELF: THE BOTTOM IS 65K.

If you bought it, hold.
If you haven't, it is time.
If you sold, leave.

>>62311487

62315328

If you got rugged, please never return to BTC. You are not financially literate, and people like you give BTC a bad rep.

I wish this entire board would fucking burn, every single asset class is twofaced and waiting for BTC to fail.

62315335
How rekt in anus is OP right about now?

62315341
>>62309503
I am gonna put my money where my mouth is as buy 15k in BTC at current price and post proof here.

item.title: If you had no other assets, just 6k in cash and you break even each month with two tenants with you in your home, and th… — 9 replies — 8.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you had no other assets, just 6k in cash and you break even each month with two tenants with you in your home, and th…

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-05 09:24 UTC

OP

If you had no other assets, just 6k in cash and you break even each month with two tenants with you in your home, and the home needs 30k of repairs (not diy stuff) to extract full value of 150-160k, would you sell for 125 and pay off the 100k HELOC or hold out? Assuming your job prospects are bleak and you're selling with no realtor just the title fees.

Early replies (first 3)

62318845
>>62318784
lol I remember this thread

is it a townhome?
did you tear out the floors?

do you understand there's only like 12 people here and we all remember your stupid ass?

62318876
>>62318845

These details are for any anons that haven't seen my question, not your bitch ass.

A lot of stuff was fucked up on the place and it wasn't stuff I'd DIY. I also did not want to live with tenants and I had a nosy neighbor who would probably report me to code for unlivable conditions.

I wasn't too keen on gambling everything on this one property, and the market in the sun belt has been softening, so I took a known exit. I just want to know what other anons would actually do in that situation.

62318887
>>62318784
Hold out for what, exactly? A miracle? A winning lottery ticket hidden behind the water heater? You're sitting on $6,000 in cash, staring at a property that needs $30,000 in real repairs, not YouTube-certified weekend-warrior nonsense, while carrying a $100,000 HELOC and facing bleak employment prospects. This isn't a position of strength; it's a position where the clock is quietly sharpening a knife.

The fantasy is that the house is "worth" $150,000–$160,000. No, it isn't. Not today. Today it's worth what a real buyer will hand you for it in its current condition. Future value doesn't pay today's bills. Potential doesn't patch roofs. Hope doesn't fund contractors.

A $125,000 offer with no realtor commission is not some outrageous lowball robbery. It's a bird in the hand while you're standing in a thunderstorm arguing about the theoretical value of birds. You'd walk away having extinguished the HELOC, removed the repair liability, eliminated the risk of vacancy, and escaped the possibility that one major surprise expense turns a difficult situation into a financial catastrophe.

Meanwhile, the "hold" strategy requires everything to go right. Tenants must stay. Repairs must somehow get funded. The job market must improve. No major systems can fail. No emergencies can occur. No unexpected expenses can appear. That's not investing; that's stacking assumptions like a tower of champagne glasses and then acting shocked when gravity shows up.

The brutal arithmetic is that being technically right about a property's future value is meaningless if you don't have the capital, income, or liquidity to survive long enough to realize it. Equity trapped behind repair costs and debt is not the same thing as cash.

In this scenario, rejecting $125,000 because the property might someday fetch $150,000–$160,000 feels less like financial discipline and more like refusing a lifeboat because the cruise ship looked more expensive on the brochure.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62318901
>>62318887

First off if this isn't an AI response, thanks

Secondly, I did have some roofing sales commissions waiting for me but after going back to work at my hourly job 6 days a week I don't have time to set leads and if they let me go then I lose my commission they owe me

I also have a couple legal battles I'm fighting where I could get some payoff but that is also undecided

So given this context, would you say the same?

62319209
>>62318876
>These details are for any anons that haven't seen my question
everyone here has seen your threads dumbass

come back in 10 years maybe you'll have a new crowd

62319265
>>62318901
its ai you dumb fuvkinh moron how can you not tell.

62319267
fucking UNBEELIEAVALE YOU MORONS CANT TELL FDUCINGN RETARDSOAHOUEHOESUGOSRGEDRPIHHIDTHIOTIH

62319270
>>62318901
>if this isn't an AI response,

62319305
>>62318901

Are you me?

62316282

You never believed in crypto. it has value. it is revolutionnary. it's worthy. You're retarded. This is the typical bear market post. Just bearish statements, then when it will inevitably resume its pump and get new highs a new wave of retards will spam bull spots at the top.

62316339
>>62316167
absolutely based 2-wheelnigger hater

62316365
>>62316282
>this post
Based

>this picture

62316385

62316402

62316469
>>62316167
tl;dr fucking faggot

62316603
>>62316167
People say this in every bear market. Last time people claimed high interest rates would kill crypto, this time it’s quantum computing and a perceived AI bubble. Bitcoin will never go away. There is too much institutional money in it now. It seems altcoins are done though. Doesn’t look like they will ever bounce back.

62318837
>>62316167
insane lows are not the end of crypto, its jews buying their next annual 2x. if something BASED is unreasonably low, and something CRINGE is unreasonably high, it is because they are exiting the old thing and entering the new one. thats why s&p pumped from 4000 to 8000, and and btc went from 130k to 65k. so you can buy their tradbags at 8k before it returns to 4k, and they can buy ur crypto at 65k before it returns to 130k

just do the opposite of whatever you think is smart

item.title: POORFAGS WIN — 11 replies — 2.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
POORFAGS WIN

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-05 06:33 UTC

OP

https://seekingalpha.com/news/4600529-which-brokerages-are-scrapping-pattern-day-trading-rules-today

Early replies (first 3)

62314898
TA is proven horseshit though.
You can't predict a future stock price by looking at yesterday's chart only.
just more guardrails getting torn off to milk retail retards.

62314901
>poorfags gain the ability to bankrupt themselves even faster
Is this really a win for them

62315034
>>62314503
I was Pattern Day trading anyways. Didn't get touched once.

Keep following the rules kid. You will definitely make it that way!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62315058
>>62314503
Ironically this is going to destroy so many broke dumbasses who think they’ll get rich from “daytrading”.

62315129
It was always retarded because it doesn't stop you from making horrible decisions. How does a daytrade limit help you when you can still buy an OTM option that expires in 24 hours? If it was really to protect poorfags from themselves, they shouldn't be allowed to touch options or any leverage. Only buy and sell.

62315299
>>62315129
>It was always retarded because it doesn't stop you from making horrible decisions.
It punishes you for making mistakes. I got flagged as a PDT when I started because I bought the two of the wrong options and then panic sold immediately. I didn't lose much money, but I got hit with a flag and couldn't day trade for 3 months.

62316057
>>62314898
Except ive done it plenty of times to predict a pump when there is absolutely no other news or catalyst other than the chart about to blast out of a consolidated triangle, its horseshit, but it works.

62316063
>>62314503
The only thing dumber than the PDT rule are retards day trading with $20k in a brokerage.

62316073
>>62315129
fuck off
let retards bankrupt themselves if they want to - they would do anyway and this allows some to make it
go live in europe if you hate freedon

62316831
To be fair the 25k rule is retarded when anyone can trade options that are capable of fucking up your account way faster.

62318741
>>62314503
So there's been a rule this whole time that outright denied you access to the market if you didn't already have some large arbitrary amount of money to piss away on day trades? Hilarious. You would think that'd be unconstitutional if this was a real country. Every stockfag's wealth should be summarily revoked.

62313595

62313964

62314023

62314048

62314204

62314317

62314711

62314748

Absolute trash.

62315125

62315140

62315517

The combined order depth before bitcoin goes to literally 0 is less than 10,000 orders, many placed by the same person.
Bitcoin literally has less people actively participating than your local Supermarket.

62318638

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 126 replies — 34.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 126 • Updated: 2026-06-05 06:46 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62310807
>>62310796
it could also be the observer effect has broken your model

62310837
>>62310807
>the observer effect has broken your model
If Saylor's found my model he could at least send me a couple shekels
That said, about 95 times out of 100 a physics-predicted pivot that happens on a weekend gets frontrun the last trading day before, which does make me suspect institutions also figured this out

62310897
>>62285204
>the thing has a ~80% hit rate over more than a year

80% of the time it works all of the time

62310926
based

62311911
Bro..?

62313065
>>62311911
Boomer stocks keep doing what they're supposed to but crypto's getting killed = this isn't market-driven, we're getting fucked

62313095
>>62285468
Tell me how to day trade using the moon phases

62313156
I want to use this but I fear I am incredibly stupid. Also why give this out for free? Isn’t the number 1 advice to keep an edge for yourself if you find one? This seems like too good to be true

62313227
>>62313156
>Also why give this out for free? Isn’t the number 1 advice to keep an edge for yourself if you find one?
It's a multi trillion dollar asset class, or at least it used to be. We're not going to move it
>I want to use this but I fear I am incredibly stupid.
Buy on confirmed green/sell on confirmed red/derisk on confirmed yellow, there's live gauges plus confirmation/invalidation on the site, and set a SL so you don't get rekt like this week when the system signaled "Buy" and everything else went up but crypto got drowned in a lake

62313294
>he's still going
Fucking based.

>>62313065
>"boomer stocks"
AI stocks literally move as fast as crypto used to lmao. This board loves Hype (or maybe 'love' is a strong word, but the 'it's gonna be the next 100 billion crypto in 2027' posters are shilling it hard) but hype is only up 4x from the lows. Meanwhile SNDK was 230 bucks in December and is now 1800 bucks. SNXX, the LETF, went up 8x from launch. The Micron LETF was 50 bucks in december and 1000 bucks now- literally 50k in that would be 1m in 5 months.

62313308
>>62313227
sorry im a bit retarded here. so you're saying this bounce continues for a few days, then back down and then pump into summer?

62313309
>>62313227
What does volume reduction mean in your trade simulator? Also that simulator is still way in beta right? Cuz according to it lower SL is always better, 0% SL bringing the best results. And leverage what already talked about but damn I thought I could become buffet starting with my 500$ portfolio for a sec

62313323
>>62313294
>AI stocks literally move as fast as crypto used to lmao.
Sometimes I wish I was in the timeline where I rotated all my ETH into NVDA in 2021. I did enough that it doesn't look like I'll need a job again, but maybe it'd be nice to have my own island

>>62313309
>Also that simulator is still way in beta right? Cuz according to it lower SL is always better, 0% SL bringing the best results. And leverage what already talked about but damn I thought I could become buffet starting with my 500$ portfolio for a sec
Extremely in beta. I added all the options (leverage, SL etc) a week ago and it's clearly fucked up the SL math. Big update's coming mid-month and it'll be fixed by then at the latest
>What does volume reduction mean in your trade simulator?
It's for the derisk on a confirmed Volatility signal. The demo strategy, in the t just cuts to 50% cash. Ofc you could just move stops up or whatever suits your own trading style

62313334
>>62313323
How much are you gonna charge for it when it’s done and how long do you think that’ll take?

62313351
>>62313334
>How much are you gonna charge for it when it’s done and how long do you think that’ll take?
Nowhere near happy enough with the site to even think about charging money for it. I'll charge for API access and the trade bot when that's done, probably this summer. Maybe a subscription for a TG channel with specific price signals? Dunno, that's all way in the future

62313359
>>62313351
And "confirmed buy/sell" means that FXwave confirms the already marked signal on the given day if I’m understanding correctly?

62314730
As a hobby, I'm putting two to three thousand AUD (5 bucks) into Bitcoin and strictly following your indicator. We'll see how it goes, moonbro.

62315151
Last week buy signal sisters how are you doing?

62316250
>>62313359
Going to try reply #3 to this to see if the jannies have actually shadowbanned me. Basically: Yes.

>>62314730
Godspeed

>>62315151
>Last week buy signal sisters how are you doing?
s-still beating buy & hold

62316626
>>62316250
Sorry for asking so many questions but you gotta get I’m skeptical. If this actually worked it‘d change my life and everytime I’ve come across some magical trading strategy that I had hopes for I only lost money. Also no you’re not shadow banned I guess

62316716
>>62316626
>Also no you’re not shadow banned I guess
Yeah, tried to send two replies last night and both times nothing posted. Weird.
>Sorry for asking so many questions but you gotta get I’m skeptical.
I appreciate the questions anon, I love talking about it, and also I was mostly expecting to have to endure a firestorm of trolling after this week's massive L so a real conversation is nice lol
This board changed my life, freed me from waging so I can spend life playing around with my autistic science experiments, so I'm here to give something back

62316751
>>62285791
Not sure if you meant this to sound so profound, the moon indeed creates the tides just like the waves of buying and selling allow us to get to the moon. It's beautiful.

62316819
>>62316716
How has this board changed your life? The only good advice I got here is "buy bitcoin if it’s low" which I definitely should follow more but it alone wouldn’t give me "financial freedom"

62317823
>>62316819
>How has this board changed your life?
LINK and ETH, officially made it on AMPL, then in addition to boomer index funds I was dumb-lucky enough to roll a bunch of ETH into NVDA in 2021 because I figured miners were going to buy every GPU forever. Wrong, it turned out, but then AI happened.

62317877
>>62284814
Honestly OP your schizo chart never had to deal with a real genuine crypto bear market like it just kept printing a 7x over 1.5 years, but some cryptos did a 10x in months during the bull run.
The next 6 months will be the real test of time.
I'm almost willing to bet that this thing will hit almost consecutive -5% stop losses.

62318004
>>62317877
>it just kept printing a 7x over 1.5 years
The demo BTC strategy is just a demonstration, to show what can be done with 0 leverage and a few trades a month, pretty much the lowest-effort and lowest-risk strategy possible using the signals, and it's at 8x while B&H is down 33%. The signals work with any trading style. One user does high lev ETH liquidation level hunts using the signals as a bull/bear bias and absolutely prints, the trade bot I'm working on right now does 4HR bollinger extrema, it all works
>The next 6 months will be the real test of time.
I agree! That said, I've been doing it for years. Dec 2024 is just when the FX layer got added, so that's where I started the count to keep everything apples to apples.
>I'm almost willing to bet that this thing will hit almost consecutive -5% stop losses.
Good thing I'm not here to take your money lol

62318299
>>62317823
When are you supposed to enter the trade though? When the daily closes? Also does that ETH liquidation trader guy have a social media presence or something I’d love to learn from him

62318509
>>62318299
>When are you supposed to enter the trade though?
The demo strategy just executes on confirmation ~2 hours after NY open. When I do perps I usually just use 4H bollinger extrema + the signals, as in Sell -> short at top of 4h BB and exit near bottom on Buy days, Buy -> long at bottom of 4h BB and exit at/near top on Sell days, small risk, and that's been consistently profitable (except this week).
>Also does that ETH liquidation trader guy have a social media presence or something I’d love to learn from him
Not that I'm aware of but it's a pretty simple strategy, he opens up a liquidation heatmap for ETH and looks at where the liquidations are. Big fat zones of stops will get hunted, so on Sell days you look for short entries around top clusters and target low clusters for TPs, on Buy days the reverse.
There's a million ways to trade on all different timeframes, from my experience everyone has an opinion on which way's best. One thing I'm going to experiment with this week is just feeding the bias and expected dates to an LLM and asking it to come up with the highest-probability entries/TPs/SL
That said even with small risk I set quite a few bucks on fire trying to knife catch this week

62318748
>>62318509
Thank you for explaining it all so well you seem like a genuinely good person. Seems like that bollinger Bands strategy won’t work for today unfortunately because it dropped so much so quickly the bands didn’t even remotely catch up. I’ll look into that liquidation heatmap strat for entries later but I’m really tired rn. I’m still super skeptical since this seems way too simple to work but I guess I could paper trade or something.

62318790
>>62318748
No problem anon
>paper trade
Really really really good idea, and journallall of it on a spreadsheet. Direction, Entry, outcome, PNL. You start to see where you're strong and weak. LLMs are great too, like an expert-level coach. Talk with them if you've got a subscription to a decent one
The two biggest things I've ever learned is never risk more than 1% on a trade, and if you lose two trades in a day pack it up and come back tomorrow
Oh, and layer buys. Never go 100% in at one price
>Seems like that bollinger Bands strategy won’t work for today unfortunately because it dropped so much so quickly the bands didn’t even remotely catch up
Absolute bloodbath. And don't try to force trades! A good entry will always come along and you can't make money with money you've lost being impatient

item.title: ZEC exploit sell — 40 replies — 22.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
ZEC exploit sell

Open thread • Replies: 40 • Updated: 2026-06-05 08:38 UTC

OP

Active ongoing Zcash exploit right now, dumping fast

Early replies (first 3)

62317133
>>62317117
You know what is funny, this whole "exploit" is fake and gay, there was only positive bews in media about zec for months.... And its the same fake shit, now they will just reverse it and it will be 100% negative news for the jews pndd it, so they can shirt it down and when they turned off the fud button in the media they can reaccumukate it and zec will be the bestest thing gigahyoerbrutal muh ultraprivacy shitcoin again..... Jews are ez!

62317139
>>62317133
In short: exit liquidity hunting the goyim.

62317152
>>62317139
+1 they are pribably the same cartel behind the hype and world coin pnds too.... As i said jews are ez, dont play their game and stay safe out in the wilderness my fellow magalanians!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62317234
>>62317220
What are you talking about? ADA is dying, sell you bags while you still can. IRS won't let you write them off.

62317241
>>62317220
You are right, i am not a trader, but i bet wld is a bice short too, since its the bestest thing since sliced bread according to cryptinigga(paid nigger shill liquidating giyims for new ferraris)

62317252
>>62317220
>shorting Balaji
ngmi

62317259
>>62317117
zcash is the total israeli scam. It was almost dead 1 year ago or so, trading for 20$.i was in the telegram and bought some around 27$. i thought i would go to 100. then the mega pump happened. 100% not organic. And now we are learning there was an exploit that allowed people running nodes to print zec, and nobody will never know because of the privacy feature KEK.
That shit is beyond scam.

62317294
>>62317117
Zioncash, we always said it.

62317295
>>62317117
>Ztrannies cry inflation bug about Monero for years
>During peak Zcash shill mania get exposed as having an infinite mint exploit
O I am laffin

62317297
>>62317259
It was sooo annoying seeing the retarded shills make money with it last year. They were right, by price, thats whats so wrong with crypto.

62317313
>ZCRASH

62317324
>>62317252
>Zcash exploit

was build in the code from the beginning!
>-26% by now, last 24h

62317494
Are the rumours true about AI cucking Zcash? Or is it just twitter trash talk

62317508
>>62317220
I am here and I trade btc because I am able to harvest outsized gains off of it in any market condition so i dont bother even looking at alts. I’ve run $144 to $832040 in just over one financial quarter.

62317975
>>62317117
How low will it fall?

62318088
>>62317117
is this bullish for my octra though

62318099
>>62317133
>this whole "exploit" is fake and gay
no it isn't, the retards designing the zero knowledge system didn't realize there was a gaping hole in their logic. these vulnerabilities sit undetected because no one bothers to verify anything these days

62318126
>>62318099
by the way, there is NO way to prove that this exploit was not used by anyone, because it is both undetectable and unlimited. that breaks every trust assumption in a zero knowledge system. in a rational world, this coin would go to zero and stay there.

62318153
>>62317117
holy fuck, why didn't I care when you posted, I could've made some money. Well I guess I'm making less money. Free short to 200 at least

62318157
>>62317975
anywhere between 200 and 60 dollars

62318176
>>62318126
$1000 EOY easy

62318272
reminder to everyone to zoom out to "all" on zcash and see how the devs started this whole shitshow with one enormous dump on the market. zcash still has the record for the largest crash of all time and it was immediately upon launch as the devs stuffed their pockets and fled. but since then there's a whole new (third) world of suckers

62318289
A new generation of privacy baggies was born. Just wait until people figure out that all of Monero's previous forks can be cracked by quantum in the future.

62318290
>>62318272
check the market cap graph

62318339
>>62318289
Only the active fork matters with XMR, by the time you deanonymize forks from years ago there is no useful adjacent information.

62318374
>>62317207
They can run a tally on flows into and out of the pool and will probably be installing a turnstile in the coming weeks. This will almost certainly either a) prove that the exploit occurred because the exploiter will move their counterfeit coins out of the pool before the turnstile can be installed, and we'll see that out > in, or b) nothing will happen, and once the turnstile is installed the worst that could happen is that the possible exploiter, who has been in a coma the past month and unable to react to the disclosure of the vulnerability, wakes up and moves some of their coins out, hitting the turnstile limit and proving that the exploit had occurred. Late migrators from the pool get scheisted and then we return to business as usual.

There is also c) which is that with Opus 4.8, mythos, etc it's conceivable that there are infinite vulnerabilities to be exploited and nobody will ever trust shielded pools and zk tech again. Same thing as is happening in slow motion with defi protocols right now. Such that even if there was never an exploit, it doesn't matter, the fact that some rando with $1000 worth of tokens and the latest version of Opus can potentially find exploits like this means game over for basically all crypto and anything else that relies on trustless execution.

62318429
oh god the zec defenders are here.... I really hope you're paid shills rather than desperate baggies, I couldn't witness that much heartbreak

62318550
>>62318429
Top Kek, fuck this kike run shitcoin that literally has a tranny behind the wheel

62318983
>>62317297
it was indeed annoying, especially if you sold early like i did. BUT deep down i knew something was off. It really felt like a complete artificial pump. It went too fast from almost dead to this is the new bitcoin. i don't like leverage but i really give some thought about shorting it, although it was a gamble seeing how it moved up randomly. Didn't go it and glad i didn't might have been scamwicked anyway even if i was right.

62318986
>>62318157
Kek my nigga, it's going back to 20$, screenshot this. That was its price 1.5 year ago by the way.

62319007
>>62318374
Anyway this goes, what a fucking disaster. Sounds like the shows over boys, the music has stopped and there are few chairs left

62319113
>>62318983
faggot

62319185
You faggots have the memory of a goldfish. Take a look at the chart, ZEC's entire history is nothing but pump and dump cycles. Nobody actually uses ZEC for its intended purpose (XMR monopolizes that use case). It has a CEO that openly schmoozes with 3-letter agencies and a hard-left trannie as lead dev, it was never going to be anything more than a glorified memecoin for whales to play with.

Keep getting dumped on, its very entertaining.
